text: This is a lady who presented with large bilateral dermoids. The technique of doing ovarian cystectomy for dermoids is same like treating any other ovarian cyst.

The surgeon opens the ovarian cortex along the anti-mesenteric border, creating a long incision approximately twelve to fifteen centimeters in length. Bilateral ovarian dermoids, each measuring twelve to fifteen centimeters, are visible.

The surgeon attempts to separate the dermoid cyst from the ovarian cortex, encountering a challenging plane of separation due to the cyst's long presence. The area appears vascular, and a weak spot in the ovarian cortex is identified during the stripping process.

The surgeon introduces an endobag into the peritoneal cavity and places the entire ovarian cyst along with the ovarian cortex inside it. The cyst may be partially emptied to debulk it before completing the cystectomy.
The surgeon performs the cystectomy inside an endobag to prevent spillage of the cyst contents, such as hair and sebum, into the peritoneal cavity, which could lead to chemical peritonitis.

Using an endobag is also extremely beneficial especially in cases where there is stage one ovarian cancer and an oophorectomy is being performed. However, in that cases it is important to take out the endobag through an incision in the vault if the patient is unmarried or is wanting fertility preservation.

The surgeon performs a cystectomy on the opposite side of the ovarian cyst, making a large incision similar to the previous side. The visible dermoid portion is carefully placed inside the endobag to prevent spillage during the procedure.

The surgeon performs a cystectomy with careful dissection to maintain the correct plane, minimizing bleeding and preserving the ovarian cortex. The surgical field shows meticulous handling to reduce the need for coagulation.

The surgeon removes the endobag containing the ovarian cyst from the peritoneal cavity. The peritoneum is then flushed with approximately five to six liters of normal saline to cleanse the area before completing the procedure.
